author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Cœlestius’ Book Which Was Produced in the Proceedings at Rome.
title: Cœlestius’ Book Which Was Produced in the Proceedings at Rome.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 5 [V.]—Cœlestius’ Book Which Was Produced in the Proceedings at Rome.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But in the book which he published at Rome, and produced in the proceedings before the church there, he so speaks on this question as to show that he really believes what he had professed to be in doubt about.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For these are his words: “That infants, however, ought to be baptized for the remission of sins, according to the rule of the Church universal, and according to the meaning of the Gospel, we confess.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For the Lord has determined that the kingdom of heaven should only be conferred on baptized persons; and since the resources of nature do not possess it, it must necessarily be conferred by the gift of grace.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now if he had not said anything elsewhere on this subject, who would not have supposed that he acknowledged the remission of original sin even in infants at their baptism, by saying that they ought to be baptized for the remission of sins?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Hence the point of what you have stated in your letter, that Pelagius’ answer to you was on this wise, “That infants are baptized with the same words of sacramental formula as adults,” and that you were rejoiced to hear the very thing which you were desirous of hearing, and yet that you preferred holding a consultation with us concerning his words.
